commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2b20b7e0413027cf1ca5ec5805f91e9a8c646381)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a pre-aborted-signal guard before `activeSession.prompt()` in the embedded agent runner and adds a focused pre-abort Vitest file.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +103. Total +118 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and `v2026.5.28` still evaluate `activeSession.prompt()` before `abortable()` sees an already-aborted signal. No live repro was run because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor-supplied current-head real behavior proof and maintainer review of whether to require production-path regression coverage before merge.

target.title: fix(attempt): prevent zombie Agent loop when abort arrives before prompt()
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: When `params.abortSignal` is already aborted before `activeSession.prompt()` is called, JS evaluates `prompt()` first (starting the async chain), then `abortable()` rejects immediately. The floating Promise creates a new `Agent._runLoop()` with a fresh `abortController` that nobody ever aborts — the Agent loops indefinitely after the attempt exits. Observed in production: 2617 LLM calls over 103 minutes from a single zombie run.
- **Why it matters**: Silent unbounded LLM cost; no user-visible symptom. Triggered deterministically by `queue.mode: interrupt` + rapid consecutive messages, and probabilistically by timeout-compaction retries.
- **What changed**: Three-layer defense against the floating-Promise zombie loop:
  1. **Pre-prompt guard** in `attempt.ts` — check `runAbortController.signal.aborted` before calling `activeSession.prompt()` and throw early, so no floating Promise is created.
  2. **`abortable()` onAbort** — call `activeSession.agent.abort()` + `clearAllQueues()` immediately when abort arrives mid-preflight (after the guard passes but before `prompt()` resolves), cancelling `Agent.activeRun` at the earliest possible moment.
  3. **`finally` backstop** — defensively `agent.abort()` + `clearAllQueues()` on attempt exit as a safety net for any edge that escapes layers 1–2.
- **What did NOT change**: No behavior change for normal (non-aborted) runs. No config added. `maxLlmCallsPerRun` hardening deferred to a follow-up per ClawSweeper's triage recommendation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #74859
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause**: `await abortable(activeSession.prompt(...))` — JS evaluates the argument `activeSession.prompt()` before `abortable()` runs its abort check. When the signal is pre-aborted, a floating Promise escapes into `Agent._runLoop()` with a fresh `abortController` that is never signaled.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: No pre-call abort state check before `prompt()`; `finally` block did not terminate an escaped Agent; `abortable()` did not proactively abort the Agent on signal.
- **Contributing context**: `void activeSession.abort()` in `abortRun()` is fire-and-forget and only aborts `agent.abortController` — which is `undefined` until `_runLoop()` starts. If abort arrives before that, the call is a no-op.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level: [x] Unit test
- Target file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.abort-before-prompt.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  1. Pre-aborted signal → `prompt()` is skipped → zero LLM calls after attempt exits
  2. Normal (non-aborted) signal → `prompt()` executes normally, not affected by the guard
  3. Abort during preflight → `abortable()` onAbort triggers `agent.abort()` → loop terminates before further LLM calls
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Uses a real `Agent` + mock `streamFn` with a call counter. No network, no gateway stack. Deterministic — the signal is already aborted at call time (case 1) or aborted during a controlled preflight window (case 3), eliminating any race.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. Aborted runs already returned immediately; this prevents hidden background activity that was invisible to users anyway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
  abort arrives → abortRun() → void activeSession.abort() [no-op, abortController=undefined]
                → activeSession.prompt() starts _runLoop, creates fresh abortController
                → abortable() rejects → attempt exits
                → _runLoop loops forever (fresh controller never aborted)

After:
  abort arrives → abortRun() → runAbortController.signal.aborted = true
                → pre-prompt guard: signal.aborted → throw AbortError (prompt() never called)
                → attempt exits cleanly
                → finally: agent.abort() + clearAllQueues() [backstop]

  abort mid-preflight → prompt() already called, activeRun exists
                      → abortable() onAbort fires → agent.abort() cancels activeRun
                      → abortable() rejects → attempt exits
                      → finally: agent.abort() [no-op, already aborted]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime: Node 22
- Model/provider: Any (bug is model-agnostic)
- Relevant config: `messages.queue.mode: interrupt`

### Steps

1. Configure `messages.queue.mode: interrupt`
2. Send a message; within < 1s send a second message
3. Observe the first run's attempt exits with `durationMs < 50ms`

### Expected

- No LLM calls after the attempt exits

### Actual (before fix)

- LLM calls continue for minutes/hours after the attempt exits; `embedded run done` never appears for the zombie

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: #74859 — zombie Agent `_runLoop` continues making unbounded LLM calls after attempt exits when `messages.queue.mode: "interrupt"` aborts a run before or during `activeSession.prompt()`.
- Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu), PR branch `fix/zombie-agent-loop-abort-before-prompt`, commit `2ccb46f0e4`, Node `v24.13.0`, pnpm `10.x`, source gateway checkout at `v2026.5.6` tag (unpatched) and PR head (patched).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm check:test-types` — 0 errors on PR files (tsgo strict mode)
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.abort-before-prompt.test.ts` — 3/3 passed in 4.06s
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` — 128 existing tests unaffected
  - Code path review: verified `abortable()` on `v2026.5.6` lacks the `agent.abort()` call in `onAbort`, has no pre-prompt guard, and has no `finally` agent cleanup — all three layers are absent, confirming the bug surface exists in the shipped release.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from PR head:
  ```
  ✓ fix: pre-aborted signal skips prompt(), no LLM calls after attempt exits (501ms)
  ✓ normal completion is unaffected: prompt() runs and LLM is called when signal is not aborted (1012ms)
  ✓ fix: abort during preflight triggers agent.abort() via abortable() onAbort, terminates before LLM call (2006ms)
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  3 passed (3)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: pre-aborted signal path produces zero LLM calls (`tracker.count === 0` after 500ms wait); mid-preflight abort path terminates Agent loop before further LLM calls (`countAfterWait === countAtExit`); normal (non-aborted) path is unaffected.
- What was not tested: live end-to-end with a real LLM provider and `interrupt` mode. Attempted local mock-provider reproduction but embedded run session/workspace prep stages exceed observation windows without a pre-configured production workspace. The bug's deterministic trigger condition (pre-aborted `AbortSignal` at `prompt()` call site) is fully exercised by the unit tests using a real `Agent` instance + mock `streamFn`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test before + passing after: `attempt.abort-before-prompt.test.ts` — 3/3 green on patched code; the pre-aborted path produces zero LLM calls after attempt exits, mid-preflight abort terminates the loop promptly.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: unit tests pass (`attempt.abort-before-prompt.test.ts` — 3/3 green); pre-aborted signal path skips `prompt()` confirmed by code review; mid-preflight abort path terminates Agent via onAbort handler.
- Edge cases checked: `agent.abort()` called on a completed run where `abortController` is `undefined` — optional chain makes it a no-op.
- What I did **not** verify: live end-to-end with a real LLM provider and `interrupt` mode (will follow up in the issue).

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `agent.abort()` in `finally` could theoretically interrupt a normally completing run if `_runLoop` hasn't cleared `abortController` yet.
  - Mitigation: `Agent.abort()` calls `this.abortController?.abort()` — when `_runLoop` completes normally it sets `abortController = undefined`, making the call a documented no-op. Covered by the "normal completion" test case.
- Risk: `agent.abort()` in `abortable()` onAbort fires while `_runLoop` is mid-iteration.
  - Mitigation: This is the intended behavior — abort the active loop. `Agent.abort()` is designed to be called concurrently; the loop checks `signal.aborted` before each LLM call.
target.author: zhumengzhu

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
